description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller monoplane with a wooden fuselage and fabric-covered wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, each bearing roundels in red, white, and blue, indicating possible French or British origin. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with a single visible pilot figure seated inside. The tail section features a conventional design with a rounded vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both adorned with matching roundel and tricolor markings. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The propeller is wooden and mounted at the nose, with visible support struts and rigging wires connecting the wings and undercarriage.
description: A vintage, single-propeller monoplane with wooden and fabric construction, open cockpit, and roundel insignia on the wings and tail. Features fixed landing gear and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the wooden propeller, open cockpit with pilot, and roundel insignia on the wings.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section with tricolor markings and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail's rounded vertical stabilizer and the rigging wires beneath the wings.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the propeller, cockpit, and the symmetrical roundel markings on both wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing monoplane with straight, unswept wings and no winglets; features external bracing wires.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers; rounded edges and tricolor markings.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit directly behind the engine; single-seat configuration with no canopy.
